competenceGoalTitle: Plan, develop and document solutions with built-in privacy and security
subGoals: 1.1 Planning: Explain how the development of a service relates to the company's strategy
1.2 Planning: Use different methods and tools in connection with planning a service
1.3 Planning: Suggest and justify idea and plan for development of a service
1.4 Development: Use standard for computer programs and rules for code language in the company
1.5 Development: Create and update database table
1.6 Development: Write code
1.7 Development: Use the company's version control
1.8 Development: Create integrations
1.9 Development: Create scripts
1.10 Testing: Use the company's procedures for user testing
1.11 Testing: Use different methods and tools in connection with user testing
1.12 Testing: Plan, justify and conduct user testing adapted to the service
1.13 Testing: Bug fixing and code review according to the company's procedures
1.14 Testing: Update database and make necessary changes when needed
1.15 Realization: Use the company's procedures for verification of service
1.16 Realization: Connect and integrate the service in professional and user environment
1.17 Realization: Collaboration with operations and project manager. See competence goal 14
1.18 Operations, maintenance and management: Collaboration with operations, project owner and contract manager.
1.19 Operations, maintenance and management: Investigate users' further needs. See competence goal 3.
1.20 Operations, maintenance and management: Continuous bug fixing and updating of database table
1.21 Entire process: Use the company's internal procedures
1.22 Entire process: Know and be able to use the company's tools and systems

competenceGoalTitle: Plan, develop and document solutions that are energy efficient and sustainable
subGoals: 2.1 Entire process: See sub-goals under competence goal 1
2.2 Explain what energy efficient and sustainable means in the development of a service
2.3 Suggest and justify relevant energy efficient and sustainable choices in the development of a service
2.4 Develop and integrate energy efficient and sustainable solutions in the service

competenceGoalTitle: Further develop a solution to meet users' needs
subGoals: 3.1 Investigate users' further needs as a knowledge base for further development of a service
3.2 Assess what can optimize the performance of the solution and suggest measures
3.3 Plan, implement and evaluate a user journey
3.4 Possibly update data processor agreement and requirements specification
3.5 In case of further development: See also sub-goals in competence goal 1

competenceGoalTitle: Plan, develop, set up and document systems for data collection, analysis and visualization
subGoals: 4.1 Use different formats for organizing and storing data
4.2 Use different tools for analysis and visualization of data
4.3 Test and use different programming languages for visualization of data

competenceGoalTitle: Plan, develop, set up and document integrations between different systems and databases
subGoals: 5.1 Plan and create a proposal for good structure in a database at the company
5.2 Facilitate and maintain enough memory space in the database
5.3 Use key performance indicators (KPIs) in their work
5.4 Make selections and justify choice of relevant data for a solution or service
5.5 Plan, set up and document integrations between different systems and databases
5.6 Use systems and databases that are available to the company

competenceGoalTitle: Develop and use documentation and guides
subGoals: 6.1 Use, collaborate and document in the company's version control
6.2 Read, navigate and use standard and guidance in the company's programs
6.3 Develop documentation and guides internally in the team, and for collaboration with, for example, the operations department
6.4 Develop guides for external users who will use solutions in the company
6.5 Stay updated on technical specifications
6.6 Document tasks, plans and projects
6.7 Find documentation and guides on the internet for reuse
6.8 Assess and justify which programs can be used to create documentation and guidance for different solutions
6.9 Document a script and explain it

competenceGoalTitle: Guide users in various tasks
subGoals: 7.1 Create and hold courses for external and internal users
7.2 Collaborate with operations on guides that users need for different solutions
7.3 Prepare technical specifications in an easy-to-understand way
7.4 Create FAQ list
7.5 Create a good layout for a guidance manual with the company's design profile. Preferably in collaboration with the communications department.
7.6 Create different forms of guidance and documentation material in user tests internally in the company

competenceGoalTitle: Handle login information in a secure and responsible manner
subGoals: 8.1 Become familiar with the company's security guidelines, and document how it is used in the work
8.2 Create and use consent forms in their work where necessary
8.3 Use the company's procedures and relevant legislation for collecting and storing personal information.
8.4 Know and be able to find GDPR and use it in the work
8.5 Reflect on and document consequences of breaches of GDPR
8.6 Suggestions: Identity and access management, Two-factor authentication, ID portal and different security levels, Single sign-on and integrate login via Azure AD

competenceGoalTitle: Develop and adapt user interfaces that meet requirements for universal design
subGoals: 9.1 Participate in the company's relevant frontend tasks
9.2 Know and quality assure the service against WCAG 2.0

competenceGoalTitle: Select and use relevant frameworks and modules for development
subGoals: 10.1 Explain the connection between different phases in a development process. This can be done in a project plan or visualized in a program.
10.2 Explore and use the company's programs, platforms and apps for development
10.3 Explore relevant programs, platforms and apps for development outside the company and suggest possible use of these internally in a project

competenceGoalTitle: Describe consequences of technical debt in solutions
subGoals: 11.1 Explain and document what technical debt is
11.2 When developing a solution: Assess whether the company can build further on an existing solution
11.3 Explain and document why reuse of data affects resources in the company
11.4 Explain and document common solution for users to avoid common debt
11.5 Explain, justify and document why reuse of data affects resources in the company, and how the company can save money on reuse
11.6 Explain and document why reuse of data affects resources in the company
11.7 Assess and document advantages and disadvantages of open source

competenceGoalTitle: Describe what requirements different solutions place on infrastructure, and what possibilities and limitations follow from the infrastructure
subGoals: 12.1 Create and document network diagram of the infrastructure
12.2 Document, test and suggest improvements to infrastructure to uncover possibilities and limitations
12.3 Document which infrastructure standards are used in the company
12.4 Explain what requirements for infrastructure have to solution based on the size of the structure in the company
12.5 Suggest and justify possible adaptations to solutions

competenceGoalTitle: Account for and apply current regulations for privacy, copyright and information security in own work
subGoals: 13.1 Know and use relevant legislation and internal policy in the work
13.2 Create and use consent forms in the work
13.3 Follow, use and suggest improvements to the company's procedures for safeguarding information security in a development process
13.4 Understand and use English-language texts and regulations that are outside the EEA
13.5 Tips for policy and regulations: Copyright Act, Data Protection Impact Assessment (DPIA), and confidentiality agreement

competenceGoalTitle: Use development strategies and collaborate with other developers
subGoals: 14.1 Participate in relevant meetings in the company
14.2 Collaborate with project owner and manager
14.3 Collaborate with internal and external parties involved in the development of a service, also across departments and companies when needed
14.4 Use the company's work methodology
14.5 Test, document, assess and share a new form of work or development method during the apprenticeship, and share it with the team

competenceGoalTitle: Debug code and fix errors in algorithms and code
subGoals: 15.1 Document use of the company's procedures for debugging
15.2 Assess how to proceed with debugging and suggest improvements
15.3 Document problems with error messages, follow up and solve them
15.4 Have knowledge of and be able to use selected programs that debug the code
15.5 Create test script
15.6 Fix code for colleagues
15.7 Understand and use deployment pipeline
15.8 Debug code and document solution

competenceGoalTitle: Explore processes related to testing and debugging in the development of solutions
subGoals: 16.1 Integration test
16.2 Participate in testing involving users
16.3 Conduct quality and functional tests used by the company
16.4 Suggestions for tests: Acceptance test, Integration test, user test internally and externally, try out automation technology in connection with debugging and testing, API or Cron

competenceGoalTitle: Handle company internal information in a secure and ethically responsible manner
subGoals: 17.1 Participate in the work of developing a requirements specification
17.2 Be introduced to Identity system for managing access to the systems
17.3 Suggestions for legislation and rules: The company's security and password policy and design manual, access control and identity system for managing access to the systems

competenceGoalTitle: Explore the concepts of machine learning and artificial intelligence
subGoals: 18.1 Discuss future needs for and consequences of machine learning and artificial intelligence with colleagues
18.2 Use algorithms to compile tables and set up a database
18.3 Map whether the company already uses machine learning
18.4 Discover if any of the solutions the company already uses offer machine learning, map, suggest and justify recommendations for further development for use in the company
18.5 Participate in courses, professional lunches, seminars or trade fairs

competenceGoalTitle: Explore and assess existing and new technologies and industry-relevant code languages
subGoals: 19.1 Stay updated on new standards, programs and programming languages
19.2 Compare what similar companies use of technology and code languages, and suggest possibilities for the company
19.3 Map and assess changes in the needs of the company's users and facilitate for this
19.4 Use cloud-specific services
19.5 Explore, map and argue for whether this can be used by the company: Integration technology, VR and AR
19.6 Argue why semantics is important in IT development

competenceGoalTitle: Reflect on and describe how technology can be misused and affect society negatively
subGoals: 20.1 Discuss how data and personal information can contribute to the spread of fake news
20.2 Write risk analysis, document and uncover attempts at fraud in the company
20.3 Familiarize with, document and preferably participate in the company's work to prevent cyber attacks on the company
20.4 At the start of a project, the apprentice can participate in discussions where advantages and disadvantages for the user are discussed

competenceGoalTitle: Account for what requirements and expectations are set for an equal and inclusive professional community, and reflect on what duties and rights employer and employee have in the training company
subGoals: 21.1 Participate in onboarding program for new employees
21.2 Document what an equal and inclusive professional community means
21.3 Document how an equal and inclusive professional community is maintained in the workplace
21.4 Reflect and document what duties and rights the employer has
21.5 Get to know your own rights as an apprentice at www.okstat.no
21.6 Reflect and document what rights and duties the employee has
21.7 Know and use the company's HSE procedures that are relevant in their work, and document this in selected tasks
